message:
Improve identifier detection, WAL-first lookups, score normalization, and job queue dedup

Query classification (query_class.rs, query_planning.rs):
- Add camelCase, PascalCase, and snake_case detection to query
  classification and identifier signal counting so that programming
  identifier queries are properly recognized and routed to code-aware
  search paths

Score normalization (blend.rs):
- Replace the hard if/else threshold (range > 0.01) in robust_normalize
  with a smooth linear blend between min-max normalized and raw scores,
  eliminating score discontinuities when the score range crosses the
  0.01 boundary

WAL-first embedding lookups (lib.rs, two_tier.rs):
- Reorder all embedding lookups (embeddings_for_doc_hashes,
  fast_vector_for_doc_id, quality_vector_for_doc_id, rescore_hits) to
  check WAL entries before on-disk index entries, ensuring recently
  upserted but not-yet-flushed embeddings are returned
- Deduplicate WAL entries during batch insert by doc_id, keeping the
  last-seen entry for each document to prevent stale duplicate vectors

Job queue deduplication (job_queue.rs, pipeline.rs, schema.rs):
- Before transitioning a job to a terminal status (Completed, Failed,
  Skipped), delete any pre-existing rows with the same (doc_id,
  embedder_id, target_status) to prevent duplicate terminal-state
  entries that could cause incorrect queue depth counts
- Extend JobState with doc_id and embedder_id fields and promote
  row_i64 to crate visibility to support the new deletion queries

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

+                let target_status = JobStatus::Failed.as_str();
+                let delete_params = [
